name: Redfish (SS-395)
events: 27 Jun 1944 With her trials and training completed USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) arrived at Pearl Harbor.
23 Jul 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in Luzon Strait.
18 Aug 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese merchant tanker Eiyo Maru (8673 GRT) in Luzon Strait in position 20°28'N, 121°04'E.

25 Aug 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese army cargo ship Batopaha Maru (5953 GRT) north-northeast of Cape Bojeador, Luzon, Philippines in position 18°31'N, 120°32'E. The damaged ship is towed to Bangui Bay, Luzon, Philippines where she is beached and declared a total loss.
16 Sep 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) torpedoed and sank the Japanese fleet tanker Ogura Maru No.2 (7311 GRT, offsite link) south of Formosa in position 21°24'N, 121°14'E.
21 Sep 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) torpedoed and sank the Japanese troop transport Mizuho Maru (8506 GRT) north of Luzon, Philippines in position 18°37'N, 120°43'E.
2 Oct 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) ended her 1st war patrol at Midway.
25 Oct 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) departed from Midway for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the east China Sea.

4 Nov 1944 At 1010 B/4, well to the west of the Mariana Islands, USS Burrfish (T/Cdr. W.B. Perkins, Jr., USN) sighted three US submarines on an opposite course. Calls were exchanged with two of them; USS Shad (T/Lt.Cdr. L.V. Julihn, USN) and USS Redfish (T/Cdr. L.D. McGregor, Jr., USN).
The third submarine sighted was USS Bang (T/Cdr. A.R. Gallaher, USN). (1)
20 Nov 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) sank a Japanese sailing vessel with gunfire of the north-east coast of Formosa in position 25°15'N, 122°23'E.
22 Nov 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) sank a Japanese sailing vessel with gunfire east of Formosa in position 24°14'N, 122°25'E.
23 Nov 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Hozan Maru (2552 GRT) east of Formosa in position 24°27'N, 122°47'E.
9 Dec 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese carrier Junyo (offsite link) east of Kyushu, Japan in position 32°13'N, 129°13'E.
19 Dec 1944 USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) torpedoed and sank the Japanese carrier Unryu (offsite link) about 200 nautical miles south-east of Shanghai, China in position 28°19'N, 124°40'E.
Redfish herself is damaged by depth charges from the escorting destroyers and is forced to terminate her patrol.
2 Jan 1945 The damaged USS Redfish (Cdr. L.D. McGregor) ended her 2nd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
6 Jan 1945 USS Redfish departed from Pearl Harbor bound for San Francisco.
6 Feb 1945 USS Redfish arrived in the Panama Canal Zone.
8 Feb 1945 USS Redfish departed the Panama Canal Zone for the Portsmouth Navy Yard.
17 Feb 1945 USS Redfish arrived at the Portsmouth Navy Yard on the US east coast for repairs and refit.
4 Jun 1945 USS Redfish arrived at Newport, Rhode Island from the Portsmouth Navy Yard for post refit torpedo trials.
5 Jun 1945 Having completed her post refit torpedo trials, USS Redfish, arrived at New London, Connecticut.
12 Jun 1945 USS Redfish, departed New London, Connecticut for the Portsmouth Navy Yard for post refit repairs.
13 Jun 1945 USS Redfish arrived at the Portsmouth Navy Yard from New London, Connecticut for post refit repairs.
2 Jul 1945 USS Redfish departed New London, Connecticut for the Panama Canal Zone.
11 Jul 1945 USS Redfish arrived in the Panama Canal Zone from New London, Connecticut.
17 Jul 1945 USS Redfish departed the Panama Canal Zone for Pearl Harbor.
30 Jul 1945 USS Redfish arrived at Pearl Harbor to prepare for her 3rd war patrol but the war was over before she could depart.
